steps.title: Attach the that clause
steps.content: The words that few authors would turn down form a restrictive relative clause describing award. A restrictive that clause attaches directly to the noun it modifies, so no punctuation should separate the noun from that.

steps.title: Remove separating punctuation
steps.content: A uses a colon after a noun phrase that is not an independent clause. C wrongly inserts a comma between award and its restrictive modifier. D uses a semicolon before a dependent clause. The remaining choice lets the modifier attach directly: an award that few authors would turn down. The answer is B.
